Schema mismatch #1: journal.json is date-grouped `[{date, sessions[]}]`, not a flat `sessions[]` as SESSION_V2 implies. Session 07 routed into the existing 2026-04-23 date group via `started_at.slice(0,10)`. The session_log handler spec makes this routing explicit.

Schema mismatch #2: lore schema uses `body` + timestamps; war-prep snippets use `rationale` + `status`. Compat shim applied: body = rationale, status preserved as extra field, created/updated set to session end. Future lore snippets should include both for renderer compat until Codex views migrate.

Schema mismatch #3: schisms have two shapes in the tree — legacy {context, volumes, rejected, chosen, reason, canon_id} and v2 {title, proposal, pros, cons, resolution, references, status}. Applied v2 fields verbatim and back-filled legacy {rejected, reason} from {proposal, resolution} for renderer compat.

Handler spec upgrades error handling from silent-skip to accumulate-and-warn — wartime telemetry cannot afford silently-dropped task_status updates.

sessions.summary: Review of sl-1-1 audit PR #3 — blocking feedback posted as COMMENT event (GitHub rejects self-account REQUEST_CHANGES; treat as changes-requested). Frame assessment: citations accurate, 'lie by omission' promotion over briefing's 'hardcoded' framing is correct, but the derived-store contract is incomplete. Abstraction assessment: under-indexed — three-state collapses _syncDisabled (local fault) into offline (network fault), the architecture formula never reads the onSnapshotsInSync drain it commits to adding, pending-count disjointness is undefined, and initial-boot (listener-not-yet-fired) state is unhandled. Five blockers plus five comment-level nits; no out-of-scope bugs filed.
decisions: Request changes (posted as COMMENT) on sproutlab PR #3; do not merge until blockers 1-5 resolved.
Answer Lyra review-ask #2 (_syncDisabled -> offline collapse): NO. Add a fourth state ('halted') with distinct copy and reload affordance; collapsing trades a pixel-lie for a semantic-lie.

Answer Lyra review-ask #3 (pending-count double-count): YES, risk is real. Resolve by disjointness contract (debounceTimers = pre-submit; hasPendingWrites across shadows = post-submit) or by leaning on onSnapshotsInSync as single source of truth.

Answer Lyra review-ask #1 (architecture contract soundness): NO. Formula must read the drain signal it lists as required (onSnapshotsInSync); must acknowledge sync.js:177 enablePersistence({synchronizeTabs:true}) — offline writes live in Firestore's IndexedDB queue, not in _syncDebounceTimers.
Flagged existing #syncToast (sync.js:970-999) as a second feedback channel the audit does not address; sl-1-2 must pick delete / unify-from-store / keep-with-divergence.
Flagged initial-boot lie: _syncReady listed as reusable but never used in derived equation; cold-start formula returns 'online' before any listener has fired.
sessions.handoff: sl-1-1 blocked pending Lyra revision. Re-review queued for next push. No out-of-scope bugs filed. Issue #25 carried the session_log payload per briefing fallback path.

sessions.summary: Fourth decree from the Capital. Three constitutional moves, filed together because they are a single constitutional frame. First: the Basilica is established as the Capital's operational hall; the five Provinces each hold an embassy within it, each embassy carrying its Province's own stylistic register. The Gates and the Visiting Chambers are retired — their functions swallowed into the embassy pattern. Second: Codex's embassy holds the Republic's running books (Constitution, Book of Law, working papers); the Archives, as a Capital room, narrow to lore and chronicles only. Third: the office of the Priest is formalized and seated with Rune, who already carries the Ritualist archetype; each embassy selects a Rite from the Twelve (as recorded in Codex) as its standing civic ritual. Implementation follows in subsequent Builder sessions; this decree is the frame.

Pre-dawn war preparation session: SproutLab cruft purge (-77,226 LOC), SEP Dashboard v2.1 baseline verified, Codex PR #19 (War Time declaration) closed with main-branch ratification noted, Temple of Mars v0.1 founded (charter + Stack B SvelteKit + 41-file scaffold + Codex campaigns.json + Session v2 schema + live GH Pages deploy at 23s after three CI hotfixes), Ignis seated as Temple Builder per Sovereign decree. The temple stands ready for War Time Hour 0.

sessions.summary: canon-proc-005-rite-lifecycle (The Rule of Institutions and Abrogations) ratified active in session s-2026-04-21-04. Six canon-law lifecycle moves formalized: Institution (instituere — any companion may petition, Priest nominates, Sovereign ratifies, new rite canon + working-paper version); Amendment (spec mutates with version bump, prior observances valid in situ); Suppression (suppressio — reversible pause, Priest or Censor proposer, observance log notes expected-absence); Supersession (newer rite replaces older, bidirectional references, historical observances retained); Abrogation (abrogatio — permanent retirement, always mints a farewell lore entry per Sovereign Q27 ruling); Dispensation (Priest-alone one-time exemption, no Sovereign escalation — the cadence authority the rung uniquely carries). Rite status enum added: instituted · suspended · superseded · abrogated (suspended is the reversible addition to the canon-status parallel). Catalog-wide constraint: every rite canon must name a machine-detectable neglect-cost signal (name-and-defer acceptable per Q30). Relationship to canon-proc-004 drawn: lifecycle moves mint decrees. Elevation path to Book V named.

decisions: Binding rule architecture: unit of binding is the invocation mode, not the role. Artifact test ('does this invocation produce a separable, attributable, auditable artifact outside the caller's transcript?') governs every binding. Yes -> subagent; no -> skill; both by context -> dual-binding. Heuristics (gating/territorial/rank-chain) are tells, not rules.
Censor dual-binding as archetypal case: Edict V final-pass as subagent, hat-switch smell-check as skill, committee delegate as subagent. Three modes, both primitives. Cipher and Nyx both dual-bound.

Sentinel as new institutional seat tied to Command Center. Quadruple-moded, dual-bound: three subagent modes (session movement log, Gates-transit log, anomaly/violation flag) + one skill mode (continuity/presence query). Profile drafting queued as todo-0028. Division of labor with Chronicler: Chronicler is historian (narrative, Republic-wide); Sentinel is security camera (raw events, CC-scoped). Watch room proposed as 15th CC room to house Sentinel.

Edict IX (UI-Canonical-Existence) proposed as altitude-setting principle: every Republic role, mode, workflow, or artifact-class must have a corresponding UI surface in the Capital that represents and audits it. Soft form (operational) now, strict form (every action is a UI button) at destination. Edict IX itself to be ratified separately; cc-022 suite's UI Manifest Cross-Reference (part 10) is the first canon cluster to declare its Manifest under Edict IX's shadow.

sessions.summary: Two-canon Builder-discipline suite ratified 2026-04-18. canon-gov-011 (Merge to Main Is the Deploy Step; Branch Pushes Are Rehearsal) responds to lore-013 'The Invisible Commit' — the cascade failure where ten feature-branch commits gave the illusion of shipped work while main sat unmerged; canon codifies the discipline that verification must reproduce the deployment path and end-of-session handoffs must distinguish rehearsal from shipment. canon-gov-012 (Android-Installed PWAs Hold a Browser-Side HTML Cache Beyond the Service Worker) ratified mid-session during the Forum Pattern application session (s-2026-04-18-01) when the Sovereign's 'Where are the new colors?' correction surfaced that Chrome's webview cache holds prior index.html independently of the registered SW; canon names the three-step deploy verification chain (merge → Pages reflects → user-device cache invalidation via pull-to-refresh) and makes cache-invalidation explicit in deploy handoffs. sessions.summary: Ran R3 composition pass on the device-sync Session 1 spec. Surfaced three findings that reshaped scope: (1) two of four 'real bugs' target greenfield reconcile code that hasn't been built, (2) the undefined cluster is two roots not one — egress normalization and ingress discrimination are asymmetric, (3) Edict III reduces to a latency question once _syncSetDocMerge becomes the architectural normalization boundary. Traced seven scenarios (listener-echo × reconcile intersection); two initial race findings (S3, S6) resolved by HR-14 sync invariant (event-loop-is-the-lock); one residual (S7 concurrent-write last-writer-wins) accepted as known limitation. Rishabh chose Option B (rebase framing) which collapsed to the event-loop lock — no explicit queue, no rebase loop needed. Spec locked at ~211 LOC net surface, filed at docs/spec-2026-04-17-aurelius-device-sync-reconcile.md in SproutLab. Handoff §4/§5 are now factually stale; spec §15 documents the delta for Lyra.
decisions: Adopt _syncSetDocMerge architectural wrapper — collapses P1R2-2 and P3R4-4 into one structural fix; Edict III reduces to debounce-vs-immediate (chose immediate)
_syncNormalizeOut deletes undefined rather than coercing to null — preserves absent-vs-present-null invariant so ingress discrimination at P4R5-7 works cleanly
MEANINGFUL_NULL_KEYS scoped single-doc only — per-entry model does not have the per-key decision point that the P4R5-7 bug class targets; tombstone semantics deferred
Month partitioning deferred from Session 1 — P7R2-2 race guard dropped; no month-doc architecture to hang it on
Reconcile isolation via HR-14 sync invariant — no await / no yield between _syncReconcileWriting set and clear; event loop is the lock

Rejected Option A (reconcile lock with explicit listener queue) in favor of Option B (event-loop-is-the-lock) — no queue needed because JS single-threading already isolates sync code from listener callbacks
S7 (concurrent-write last-writer-wins) accepted as known limitation, user-facing mitigation text drafted for future user docs
L529 per-entry update uses inline _syncNormalizeOut rather than separate _syncUpdate wrapper — .update() semantics already merge; one wrapper covers set({merge:true}) correctness
Spec filename convention adopts author-in-filename (spec-YYYY-MM-DD-author-subject.md) following the handoff naming pattern
sessions.handoff: Spec R3-locked and filed. sessions.summary: The Codex RPG Design Session — the single longest and most consequential design session in Codex history. Transformed Codex from a library-themed project tracker into a full personal civilization engine with RPG architecture. Designed 16 entity types across 4 layers, a 6-tier Aura rarity system, 3 Architect paths with 12 hybrid classes, 7 Domain levels, 17 AI companions (The Immortal Order), Mortal Companion dossiers, Ink economy, Entropy antagonist, The Quiet burnout detection, The Sanctum spatial home screen with 11 rooms, daily mini games, seasonal events, the Narrator voice, and a 20-phase implementation plan. Produced a 57-page dissertation PDF (codex-dissertation.pdf) as the canonical seed document. Four design mockup iterations (v1–v4) were built, each improving on the last: v1 established the dark sanctum aesthetic, v2 added particle effects and new screens, v3 integrated real repo data (39 canons, 5 volumes, 31 chapters), v4 addressed Aura visual rendering and text sizing. Key design discovery: Aura must be felt (visual atmosphere) not read (text labels). Key philosophical foundation: The Three Pillars — Nothing Is Wasted, The Map Is Not the Territory, Growth Is Fractal Not Linear.
decisions: Lore has 5 categories: Edicts, Origins, Cautionary Tales, Doctrines, Chronicles — names chosen to match the library/ancient-knowledge theme
Prophecies have progressive revelation — waypoints hidden until progress thresholds reached, creating discovery not backlog anxiety
Prophecies have branching paths with hidden branches that reveal at certain waypoints
Prophecy lifecycle: Foretold → Active → Fulfilled/Defied/Suspended — Defied auto-generates Cautionary Tale, Fulfilled auto-generates Origin
Scrolls have 3 origin types (Authored, Acquired, Revealed) and 3 mastery levels (Studied, Practiced, Internalized)
Aura system: Iron → Bronze → Silver → Gold → Amethyst → Obsidian with hard distribution caps (unlimited, 30%, 20%, 10%, 5%, 2%)
Aura is proposed by Codex (computed from Bond count, lineage depth, domain spread) and confirmed by Architect
Aura is visual atmosphere (gradients, shadows, glows, accent lines), NEVER text labels
3 Architect paths: Maker (Craft), Sage (Insight), Sovereign (Influence) — forking at Level 5
12 Hybrid classes emerge at Level 15 from primary path × secondary stat intersection
Transcendent class auto-generated at Level 25 from unique combination of path + hybrid + domain + prophecy + seal data
17 Companions in the Immortal Order: 4 Builders, 3 Strategists, 4 Seekers, 3 Guardians, 3 Wildcards
Companion ranks: Stranger → Acquaintance → Ally → Confidant → Bonded → Soulbound
Mortal Companions: deep dossiers of real people with communication style, decision speed, motivation drivers, friction points, autonomy level
Ink is spendable currency earned proportionally to XP — spent on Aura petitions, Companion gifts, Sanctum customization, Prophecy acceleration, Seal hints
Entropy is the silent antagonist: Scroll Decay, Domain Rust, Companion Drift, Canon Erosion, Prophecy Fog, Lore Sedimentation
The Quiet: burnout detection that halves XP requirements, pauses side quests, dims the Sanctum, and waits patiently — never punishes
The Sanctum: 11 rooms unlocked progressively — Scriptorium, Stacks, Chronicle Hall, Observatory, Forge, Ember Garden, War Table, Companion Quarters, Vault, Atlas Chamber, Inner Sanctum
Dawn Page is the daily arrival — Whisper, Rite candles, Active Fires, Companion greeting, one mini game. Dusk Reflection is the evening counterpart.
The Binding: annual narrative auto-compiled from the year's Prophecies, Relics, Lore, Rites, Echoes — reads like an autobiography chapter
Side Quests: 7 types — Bounties, Errands, Challenges, Mysteries, Pilgrimages, Diplomatic Missions, Scroll Expeditions
8 Daily Mini Games: Whisper Response, The Forge, Archive Roulette, Pip's Riddle, Domain Duel, Cartographer's Game, Echo Forecast, Companion's Question
4 Seasonal Events: Great Cataloguing (quarterly), The Reckoning (epoch transition), Ember Storm (luminary trigger), Prophecy Convergence (rare)
The Three Pillars established as philosophical foundation: Nothing Is Wasted, The Map Is Not the Territory, Growth Is Fractal Not Linear
Minimum text size: 9px floor across entire app — daily-use demands eye comfort
Sound is opt-in by default, with per-room ambiance, per-event sounds, and per-companion audio signatures
20-phase implementation plan estimated at 50–70 sessions (4–6 months)

Added 9 new functions: _renderDetailEmpty() (shared empty-state HTML), _restorePanelWidth() (per-tab width persistence via regFilter.desktopPanelWidths), _initDragHandle() (mousedown+touchstart with double-bind guard via dataset.dragInit), renderRegisterTable() (first-render wrapper injection + re-render master-only update), renderIMTable() (same pattern for IM), _renderRegView()/_renderIMView() (dispatchers: desktop→table, mobile→list), _onDragMove() (clamp 280px master/320px detail), _onDragEnd() (persist ratio, reset cursor). Replaced 19 call sites across 6 files (events.js, invoice-ops.js, state.js, im-form.js, im.js, tabs.js) — all renderRegisterList()/renderIMList() calls now route through dispatchers, except switchTab which uses explicit desktop/mobile branches. renderRegister() top-level entry point gets desktop shortcut. 4 global drag listeners (mousemove/mouseup/touchmove/touchend) registered once in init.js. decisions: Drag handle width (master.style.width) is the acknowledged HR-1 inline style exception — dynamic drag widths cannot be class-based
